summary: This notebook implements a Double Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent for the Lux AI Season 3 competition. It defines a custom PyTorch MLP architecture, a replay buffer, and an off-policy training loop using the `luxai-s3` Gym environment. The state representation encodes local unit context (position, energy, closest relic, enemy proximity, and normalized step), while the action space covers movement and a "sap" attack. The author trains the agent against a random baseline, logs progress with Weights & Biases, and packages the trained model into a Kaggle submission agent. Readers should understand how to structure an RL agent for a grid-based strategy game, manage state/action spaces, and handle training-to-inference conversion for competition submissions.
competition_flow: The notebook initializes the Lux AI Season 3 Gym environment, trains a Double DQN agent via off-policy updates against a random opponent, saves the PyTorch model weights, wraps the inference logic in a Kaggle submission agent, and packages it for upload.
models: Double DQN (custom MLP: 2 hidden layers of 220 units, ReLU, linear output)
insights: Double DQN is used to mitigate Q-value overestimation by decoupling action selection from evaluation.
The state representation deliberately limits scope to local, immediately relevant features to keep the network tractable.
Epsilon decay and periodic target network updates are critical for stabilizing off-policy learning in this environment.
